target_emits.name: create_auth_server
target_emits.purpose: Create the auth-server in the Library. The preprocessing normalizer flattens either a rad_server (type RADIUS) or a tacacs_server (type TACACS) record into a common _<field> shape; the per-field key_mappings below map that onto the Central auth-server body. Optional fields the operator never set (or that don't apply to the source type) are dropped so Central applies its own defaults.
target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST per source rad_server / tacacs_server record.

target_emits.name: assign_auth_server
target_emits.purpose: Assign the auth-server profile to the target scope so the Mobility Gateway can reference it from its server-groups / aaa-profile. Multi-device-function array packed into a single POST.

config-assignment: {one entry per device_function in target_meta.device_functions: { scope-id, device-function, profile-type='auth-servers', profile-instance='{name}' }}

target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST; all device-functions share the array.

target_meta.device_function_filtering_rule: AOS 8 auth servers back gateway-terminated (tunnel-mode) WLAN auth; the AOS 8 -> Central migration targets MOBILITY_GW (the auth-server is referenced by server-groups, which are referenced by the gateway aaa-profile). Operators with AP-direct (bridge) auth can override device_functions in runtime_values.

target_scope_id_resolution.rule: Central scope_id is supplied by the consuming skill at runtime, derived from the AOS 8 rad_server / tacacs_server record's binding scope.
target_scope_id_resolution.input: Source binding scope (string path / identifier); skill-specific

depended_by.notes: An AOS 8 server_group_prof lists member auth servers by name (auth_server[].name). The central:server_group translation emits a server-group body whose servers[].server-name reference these auth-servers; the referenced auth-server must exist in Central before the server-group POST. This translation creates the prerequisite auth-server objects (and they are in turn referenced by the gateway aaa-profile).

objects.notes: REST schema 'rad_server' (CLI noun: aaa authentication-server radius). 28 fields per the vendored AOS 8 OAS; every value is wrapped one level deep (rad_host:{host}, rad_authport:{authport}, rad_timeout:{timeout}, ...). The preprocessing normalizer unwraps the migration-relevant subset and tags _type=RADIUS. rad_key.key is the shared secret (PII — see draft_notes).

objects.live_shape_example: (zero records in maintainer's tenant; field set taken from the vendored AOS 8 OAS /object/tacacs_server schema)
objects.notes: REST schema 'tacacs_server' (CLI noun: aaa authentication-server tacacs). 10 fields per the OAS (tacacs_host:{host}, tacacs_key:{key}, tacacs_tcpport:{tcp-port}, tacacs_timeout:{timeout}, tacacs_retransmit:{retransmit}, ...). The normalizer tags _type=TACACS and maps the single tacacs_tcpport into the shared _auth_port slot. NOT live-verified (no TACACS servers configured on the maintainer's tenant) — validate the Central TACACS port/field names via central_translation_preview against a tenant with TACACS configured.

name.to: Central auth-server 'name' (path param step 1, profile-instance step 2, body 'name')

type.notes: Set by the normalizer: rad_server -> RADIUS, tacacs_server -> TACACS. Central enum also allows LDAP / WINDOWS / RFC3576 / RADSEC / LOCAL (out of scope here).

secret.notes: Required. rad_key.key / tacacs_key.key. PII — see draft_notes; tokenization is handled by the redaction middleware once the AOS-CX secret rule ships (prereq PII PR).

auth_port.notes: RADIUS rad_authport.authport (default 1812) OR TACACS tacacs_tcpport.tcp-port (default 49). Dropped when operator-default so Central applies its own default. Central field 'auth-port' live-verified on a RADIUS server; TACACS reuse of the same field not yet verified against a TACACS-configured tenant.

acct_port.notes: RADIUS rad_acctport.acctport (default 1813). None for TACACS. Central field 'acct-port' live-verified.

enable_radsec.notes: RADIUS radsec_enable (AOS 8 empty-object flag -> bool True when set; dropped otherwise).

radius_server_mode.notes: Set to AUTH_AND_COA by the normalizer when a co-located RFC 3576 (CoA) server is correlated from runtime_values['coa_servers'] (aaa_prof.rfc3576_client[]) by matching IP. Omitted otherwise -> Central defaults to AUTH. RADIUS only (issue #322).

dynamic_auth.notes: Set True alongside radius-server-mode=AUTH_AND_COA when a co-located CoA server is correlated. Central folds CoA onto the RADIUS server (no separate RFC3576 server object / CoA secret field — it reuses shared-secret-config). #322.

unmapped_fields.reason: Inheritance/system markers are AOS 8-internal metadata, not body content (consumer filters _flags.inherited/default at definition scope). The *_clone field is a CLI authoring convenience (copy-from-existing), not a server attribute.

unmapped_fields.from: rad_req_mod_auth / rad_req_mod_acct (attribute-modifier profile refs), rad_called_station_id, cppm_username_password
unmapped_fields.reason: Attribute modifiers reference separate Central attribute-profile objects (own translation, not authored yet). called-station-id and CPPM username/password have candidate Central counterparts but are not live-verified; defer to a follow-up rather than emit unverified field names. cppm_username_password.cppm_password is additional PII to cover in the PII PR.

unmapped_fields.from: rad_mac_delimiter / rad_mac_lowercase / use_md5 / rad_service_type_framed_user / calling_station_use_ip / ipv6_enable / rad_mode / rad_srcvlan_ip6addr / radsec_{client,ca,server}_cert / tacacs_authorization / tacacs_mode
unmapped_fields.reason: Advanced / controller-plumbing knobs. Some have Central equivalents (mac formatting, service-type) but field names are unverified; source-interface (srcvlan) is controller plumbing that dissolves at migration; radsec cert refs require separate cert management. Map per-field once verified against a configured sample.

ignored_variants.reason: AOS 8 may surface built-in auth-server placeholders. Consumer filters _flags.default / _flags.system / _flags.inherited (at definition scope) BEFORE calling emit_calls, matching central:net_group / central:role convention.

draft_notes: v1 translation, Gateway-targeted. POST /auth-servers/{name} then config-assignment to MOBILITY_GW. ONE translation handles both rad_server (RADIUS) and tacacs_server (TACACS) via the aos8_auth_server preprocessing normalizer — mirrors how central:net_group handles netdst + netdst6.

TEMPLATE COMPLIANCE: standard Paradigm-B (per-field key_mappings + structurally complete body). The rad_*/tacacs_* divergence + one-level value wrapping is flattened by the normalizer (translations/preprocessing/aos8_auth_server.py); the JSON stays thin.

PII: rad_key.key / tacacs_key.key (shared secret) and cppm_username_password.cppm_password pass through cleartext (live-confirmed: rad_key came back cleartext on the maintainer's tenant). The redaction middleware does NOT yet tokenize the AOS-CX auth-server secret shape — a prerequisite PII PR (extend redaction/rules.py; RADIUS secret -> RAD kind, TACACS secret -> TACACS kind per the CoA/RAD family convention) MUST land before enabling ENABLE_CENTRAL_WRITE_TOOLS on a tenant with untrusted AI clients.

DEPENDENCY: central:server_group references auth-servers by name (servers[].server-name). Run this translation BEFORE central:server_group, which runs before the gateway aaa-profile / wlan-ssid translations.

CoA (RFC 3576) CORRELATION (#322): AOS 8 has NO standalone CoA-server object — CoA servers are referenced from aaa_prof.rfc3576_client[] (each entry: rfc3576_server/server_ip + rfc3576_secret + replay/timestamp/window). Central has no separate RFC3576 server in practice either; it folds CoA onto the co-located RADIUS auth-server as radius-server-mode=AUTH_AND_COA + dynamic-authorization-enable (live-verified on sys_central_nac). The consumer passes the collected aaa_prof.rfc3576_client[] entries via runtime_values['coa_servers']; the normalizer matches by IP (rfc3576_server == rad_host.host) and sets the AUTH_AND_COA mode on that RADIUS server. coa_servers is OPTIONAL runtime context (absent => plain AUTH).

CoA SECRET CAVEAT (#322): AOS 8 keeps separate secrets — rad_key (RADIUS) and rfc3576_secret (CoA) — but Central has ONE shared-secret-config per auth-server. When the co-located CoA secret MATCHES the RADIUS key, this is lossless. When they DIFFER, Central cannot express both; v1 keeps the RADIUS rad_key and the divergent CoA secret must be reconciled by the operator. The consumer/skill should surface a mismatch as an OPERATOR-MAP finding (compare rfc3576_secret.key vs rad_key.key during correlation).

FIELD NAMES live-verified against the tenant: auth-server-address, shared-secret-config{secret-type:PLAIN_TEXT, plaintext-value}, auth-port, acct-port, timeout, nas-identifier, enable-radsec, radsec-port, radius-server-mode, dynamic-authorization-enable. NEEDS-PREVIEW still: the config-assignment profile-type ('auth-servers'), whether an auth-server needs its own scope assignment vs being referenced indirectly via server-groups, and the TACACS port/field mapping (no TACACS server configured on the maintainer's tenant).

_flags.default HANDLING: the normalizer drops sub-values flagged _flags.default so Central applies its own defaults rather than us pinning AOS 8's resolved defaults; required fields (name/host/secret/type) are always carried.
Idempotency: a SHARED POST for an existing auth-server name returns HTTP 409; consumer must handle 409 idempotently.
